content:
Thanks for the contribution, it looks really interesting.
Not sure what a good strategy will look like (capturing some but not all prey helps your predators because they don't have to avoid their predators as much anymore) - I guess we will find out.

Skimming through the code, it looks clean and readable, which is nice as well.

I suggest adding tooltips with coordinates to each cell. Right now you give the position for each elemental, but the rest has to be done by counting.

When you MOVE the same unit id twice, you lose the game. On CodinGame it's common to give a warning and ignore the action, as long as it's syntactically correct.

For a perfectly symmetric map you would need a hexagon grid. Not saying that you should do that, it would make catching too hard I think.

Order of explanation
* The overall readability would benefit from reordering the sections for a more logical flow of mechanics: Actions => Movement => Captures => Prisoners.

* Reordering would also allow you to remove some redundancy, particularly the first paragraph in the current Movement section.

Actions
* Please format "x" and "y" in "(x, y)" using <var> tags. You may keep parentheses/comma unformatted or continue to wrap them in <const> if preferred.

Movements
* Please clarify whether movement is:
  - 4-directional (N, S, E, W), or
  - 8-directional (including diagonals).

* Please specify a deterministic rule (e.g. fixed direction priority order) when multiple shortest paths exist.

Capture
* Please specify whether anything happens when prey enters an enemy camp.

Game End
* "At least one player has all their elementals prisoner.": Needs a wording fix. Suggested rewrite:

At least one player has all of their elementals captured as prisoners.

Technical Details
* "The score of a team is the number of prisoners belonging to its prey team.": Sounds slightly awkward. Suggested rewrite:

The score of a team is the number of prisoners it has captured.

Inputs
* Please state in the following values are integers in the relevant descriptions:
  - mapSize
  - elementsCount
  - elementalId
  - x
  - y

Inputs / Default stub
* Please change "prisoner" from bool to int, as some language stubs don't provide a bool to the player (e.g. Lua, Pascal). Use 0 / 1 instead of false / true.

Output
* Please format "x" and "y" in "(x, y)" using <var> tags. You may keep parentheses/comma unformatted or continue to wrap them in <const> if preferred.

Constraints
* Consider clarifying camp distribution:
  - Does each player always have exactly one camp?
  - Is the number of camps and elementals guaranteed to be equal for all players?

Leagues
* Do you plan to introduce additional mechanics in later leagues? For example, the story mentions rescuing elementals; will that be implemented?

Referee / Viewer
* I'll perform some testing later.

Thanks.

Les règles me semblent claires.
Le jeu me plaît.
Mais il manque le boss dans l'arène pour passer à la ligue suivante.
Du coup je ne peux tester que le niveau de base.

Hi everyone,

I've just published a Work In Progress version of my multiplayer game Elemental Wars.
It's a 3-player tactical game based on a rock-paper-scissors dynamic (Water / Fire / Plant), with simultaneous captures and simple pathfinding-based movement.

At this stage, the core mechanics are in place:
- movement toward a target (1 step per turn),
- simultaneous captures,
- prisoners system,
- (later leagues) release mechanics.

I'm mainly looking for feedback on:
- clarity of the rules / statement,
- gameplay feel and balance,
- edge cases or unclear situations,
- overall fun factor

Don't hesitate to try it out and share your thoughts even early impressions are very helpful!
Thanks

Merci pour ton retour !

Tu as raison il n'y a pas encore de boss dans l'arène car nous ne sommes que 2 joueurs pour l'instant...
Il devrait apparaître dès qu'on sera un peu plus nombreux.

En attendant, tu peux tester les boss en changeant de ligue en haut à droite.
Ils sont bien présents et adaptés aux mécaniques de chaque niveau.

N'hésite pas si tu vois autre chose.

Thank you very much for the detailed review!

All the points have been addressed:
- sections reordered (Actions > Movement > Captures > Prisoners)
- redundant parts removed
- movement clarified (4 directions + deterministic rule)
- capture behavior clarified (including in camps)
- Game End and Technical Details reworded
- inputs standardized (integers, prisoner as 0/1)
- formatting fixed (<var> x, y)
- camp distribution clarified

Regarding leagues, there is a clear progression already implemented:
- League 1: 1 elemental per team (onboarding)
- League 2: multiple elementals per team
- League 3: prisoner release system

Thanks again for the review, I'll be happy to follow up after referee/viewer testing!

Thank you for your quick update. I've reviewed the updated contribution and done some testing. Below are my comments:

Captures
* Please clarify whether prisoners can take part in captures. For example:
  - Turn 1: A Water elemental captures a Fire elemental. The Fire elemental is moved to the Water camp as a prisoner.
  - Turn 2: A Plant elemental enters the Water camp. Will it be captured by the Fire elemental prisoner?

Prisoners and releases (Level 3)
* Please clarify: when prisoners are released, is this purely a status change (i.e. [[prisoner]] switches from 1 to 0), with the units remaining on the predator's camp until moved on a later turn?

* (Observation, not a request for change) It appears that an elemental captured during a turn could be released in the same turn if a liberator moves onto the predator's camp during that turn.
Could you confirm whether this interpretation is correct?

Constraints
* Since each team always has exactly one camp, you may simplify the wording by removing "the same number of camps and".

* For Level 1, please state explicitly that [[elementalsCount]] = 3 rather than giving a range of 3 to 15.

* Consider rearranging the constraints into a more logical grouping and order.

Leaderboard
* It appears that submissions across all three leagues are stuck at 0% battle progress. You may want to report this as a potential bug on the Discord server.

Referee
* The statement specifies that commands should be separated by |, but the referee code appears to split commands using semicolons (;).

Thanks.

All the points you raised have been addressed.
I confirm that capture/release within the same turn is possible.

The league progression and associated mechanics are now clearly explained.

Regarding the leaderboard, I currently only see 1 or 2 players per league.
Since the game requires exactly 3 players, this likely explains why matches haven't started yet.

Thanks again for your help, and let me know if anything else comes up!

The updated statement looks good overall. The additional league information is especially helpful; it gives players a clear sense of what to expect in higher leagues. Nicely done.

Introduction
* The wording "first Boss" is used in all levels, but it applies to Level 1 only. Consider removing "first" from all levels to avoid confusion.

* Please remove "and unlock new opponents", as this game doesn't feature new opponents.

Map
* Please add the following to make it clear that out-of-bound movement or checks need not be handled:
All border cells of the grid are walls.

Prisoners
* "ignored by the capture resolution": Consider replacing "by the" to "during" for smoother wording.

Constraints
* In Levels 2 and 3, consider mentioning that [[mapSize]] is always odd, or just listing the possible values.

Default stub
* Please update the remark on output format to reflect that the delimiter is now a semicolon instead of a pipe.

Leaderboard
* Understood. Your explanation makes sense.

Thanks.
